expressions.english: karaoke, singing room
love song
singing; song; sound of music
exciting song; cheerful song
expressions.notes: 신나는 is the adjective form of 신나다.

expressions.english: to listen to a song
to sing a song
expressions.notes: 부르다 = to call (one's name); to sing

expressions.english: to be not good at singing
to hum
Do you want to go to karaoke?
expressions.notes: -(으)ㄹ 래요? = Do you want to + V?

expressions.english: Do you know the title of this song?
I think this song is good.
expressions.notes: - 것 같다 = It seems/looks like + S + V

expressions.english: Please turn the music volume up.
expressions.notes:
키우다 = to raise
소리를 키우다 = to turn up the volume

expressions.english: Do you like singing?
Please recommend some cheerful songs.
He sings very well.
expressions.notes:
정말 = really; very
잘하다 = to be good at something

expressions.korean: 그 가수는 춤은 잘 추지만 노래는 잘 못해요.
expressions.english: He dances really well, but can't sing well.
expressions.notes:
가수 = singer
춤 = dance
추다 = to dance

expressions.korean: 학교 가는 길에 버스에서 항상 노래를 들어요.
expressions.english: I always listen to songs on the bus on my way to school.
expressions.notes:
길 = road, street; way
-가는 길 = on one's way to + place
버스 = bus

expressions.korean: 저는 노래를 정말 못하니까 노래 시키지 마세요.
expressions.english: I really can't sing, so don't make me sing.

expressions.korean: 기분이 좋아서 저도 모르게 노래를 흥얼거렸어요.
expressions.english: I felt so happy that I was humming without even noticing it.
expressions.notes:
기분 = feelings; mood
모르게 = without knowing; without meaning to + V

expressions.korean: 저는 노래는 잘 못 부르지만 노래방에서 노래를 부르는 거는 좋아해요.
expressions.english: I can't sing very well, but I do like singing at karaoke.
